# Stitch Skill — LLM Reference

AI UI generation skill. Generates/updates/iterates HTML screens via Google Stitch API.
All commands: `node ~/.claude/skills/stitch/stitch.mjs <command> [args]`
API key: `~/.config/stitch/key` (auto-loaded, never set in env)
Output: `design/stitch/` in project root — manifest.json + <name>.html + <name>.png

## Decision tree — always use `auto`, never pick mode manually

```
manifest.json missing?         → stitch init --auto  (then confirm with user)
--screen hint + exists         → UPDATE
prompt names existing screen   → UPDATE
prompt = 2+ screens            → BREAKDOWN → generate each
default                        → GENERATE
```

## Commands

```bash
stitch init --auto                                      # read docs, propose style config
stitch init --aesthetic "X" --brand "X" --palette "X"  # apply confirmed config
stitch auto "<intent>" [--screen <name>]                # auto-picks mode
stitch generate "<prompt>"                              # new screen
stitch update <name> "<change>"                         # edit existing screen (targeted)
stitch breakdown "<full app: login, dashboard, ...>"    # multi-screen generation
stitch variants <name> [--range REIMAGINE|EXPLORE|REFINE] [--count N] [--aspects LAYOUT,COLOR_SCHEME]
stitch list                                             # show manifest
stitch status                                           # check files on disk
```

## First use in a new project (no manifest.json)

1. Run `stitch init --auto` — reads CLAUDE.md, README.md, PRD.md, docs/**, package.json
2. Output includes DOCS_READ, REASONING, PROPOSED_CONFIG, ready-to-run init command
3. Present proposal to user with reasoning, ask for confirmation/adjustments
4. Run confirmed `stitch init --aesthetic "..." --brand "..." --palette "..."`
5. If PROPOSAL_RESULT: no_docs — ask user 3 questions: aesthetic, brand reference, palette

## init flags

```
--aesthetic   "dark minimal"          Visual anchor (encodes color+type+mood)
--brand       "similar to Linear"     Design reference (one phrase = full style package)
--palette     "deep slate, teal"      Color palette description
--typography  "Satoshi, Geist Mono"   Typography spec
--profile     product|marketing|dense Quality profile (default: product)
--theme       "Light, modern"         Theme description
--platform    "Web, Desktop-first"    Platform (auto-detected if omitted)
--notes       "..."                   Extra constraints on all prompts
```

## Prompt quality rules (applied automatically by prompt-builder)

- Every generate prompt outputs: vibe statement + DESIGN SYSTEM block + Avoid constraints
- Every update prompt: targeted change + "Please refrain from altering any other functionalities or design elements"
- Prompt limit: 4500 chars (Stitch truncates at 5000 and silently drops components)
- One change per update — never bundle layout + content + style changes

## Variants creative range

```
REIMAGINE  dramatic, experimental — use first on new screens to find direction
EXPLORE    moderate variation — default
REFINE     conservative polish — use when near-final
```
Workflow: REIMAGINE first → pick best direction → REFINE to production quality

## Profiles → style vocabulary

```
marketing  editorial, asymmetric grids, expressive type, scroll reveals
product    modern minimal, card-based, focused, 150-200ms transitions
dense      functional minimal, table-first, zero animation, monospace data
```

## Limits

- Free tier: 350 Gemini Flash / 50 Gemini Pro generations per month
- Generation time: 2–10 minutes per screen
- No persistent style memory in Stitch — style context injected on every prompt by prompt-builder
- Variants: 1–5 per call

## Output structure

```
design/stitch/
├── manifest.json              # projectId, defaultDevice, styleConfig, screens[]
├── <name>.html                # latest HTML (never edit manually)
├── <name>.png                 # screenshot
└── <name>.v<n>.html           # archived previous versions (never delete)
```

## manifest.json schema

```json
{
  "projectId": "stitch-project-id",
  "defaultDevice": "DESKTOP|MOBILE|TABLET|AGNOSTIC",
  "styleConfig": {
    "aestheticAnchor": "...", "brandReference": "...", "colorPalette": "...",
    "typography": "...", "profile": "product|marketing|dense",
    "theme": "...", "platform": "...", "notes": "..."
  },
  "screens": [{
    "id": "screen-id", "name": "login", "prompt": "original prompt",
    "deviceType": "DESKTOP", "version": 1, "updatedAt": "ISO date",
    "htmlPath": "design/stitch/login.html", "imagePath": "design/stitch/login.png"
  }]
}
```

## Design system contract

- Check manifest before implementing any UI component
- Extract colors/spacing/layout from generated HTML before writing component code
- Never invent UI patterns that contradict a Stitch screen
- design/stitch/ is append-only via this skill — rule in rules/design-system.md
